text:
Here is a roundup of the latest major news regarding OpenAI as of mid-2024:

**1. Apple and OpenAI Partnership**
At Apple’s WWDC 2024 in June, it was officially announced that OpenAI is integrating ChatGPT into iOS 18, iPadOS 18, and macOS Sequoia. This integration will allow Siri to hand off complex requests to ChatGPT (using the GPT-4o model). Notably, Apple stated that users will not need an OpenAI account to use the free tier of this integration, and privacy protections will be in place (IP addresses are redacted, and requests aren't stored). 

**2. Launch of GPT-4o**
In May 2024, OpenAI announced GPT-4o ("o" for omni). This new flagship model is significantly faster than previous versions and can reason across text, audio, and video in real-time. A major highlight of GPT-4o is its improved voice capabilities, allowing for much more natural, conversational interactions without the awkward delays present in previous voice modes. 

**3. Leadership and Departures**
OpenAI has experienced significant changes in its executive team over the last few months:
* **Departures:** Jan Leike (alignment and safety co-lead) and Ilya Sutskever (co-founder and Chief Scientist) announced their departures in May 2024. They have since co-founded a new AI safety startup called Safe Superintelligence Inc. (SSI). John Schulman, a co-founder and lead of reinforcement learning, also recently left OpenAI to join rival AI company Anthropic. Additionally, Greg Brockman (co-founder and President) announced he is taking a sabbatical until the end of 2024. 
* **New Appointments:** In May 2024, OpenAI appointed retired U.S. Army General and former Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, Paul M. Nakasone, to its Board of Directors to help oversee safety and security. 

**4. Disbanding the Superalalignment Team**
Following the departures of Jan Leike and Ilya Sutskever, OpenAI dissolved its "Superalignment" team—a team dedicated to ensuring that future highly advanced AI systems remain aligned with human values and safety. OpenAI stated that the team's work will be integrated into the broader company research efforts, but the move drew criticism from some AI safety advocates.

**5. Watermarking AI-Generated Images
In May 2024, OpenAI announced it is joining the Steering Committee of C2PA (Coalition for Content Provenance and Authenticity). As part of this, OpenAI will add metadata (a cryptographic watermark) to images generated by DALL-E 3 and its newer Sora video model. This is part of an industry-wide effort to help people identify AI-generated content and combat deepfakes in the lead-up to global elections.

**6. Restructuring the Board of Directors**
OpenAI has continued to reshape its board following the November 2023 leadership crisis. In recent months, the company added new board members including Paula Goldman (Senior Vice President of Salesforce) and Nicole Seligman (former President of Sony Entertainment). Along with the addition of General Nakasone, the expanded board helps guide the company's governance and safety initiatives.

To stay completely up to date on smaller, daily developments, checking OpenAI's official blog or technology news sites like The Verge and TechCrunch is recommended, as OpenAI frequently pushes minor updates and beta features to their API and apps.
